capability_statement: AOF can present a provider-backed action as one controlled execution candidate with go/no-go status and a production-not-authorized boundary.
before_state: Provider readiness evidence existed across many artifacts and had to be mentally assembled.
after_state: Operator can inspect one controlled execution candidate and audit result before go/no-go.
scenario: AOF is considering a bounded GitHub issue creation provider-backed action.
five_minute_demo: Run provider-controlled-execution-candidate-audit and inspect PCEC-TASK-114-V94 to see upstream refs, stop conditions, and production authorization=false.
time_saved_or_work_reduced: No manual assembly of approval, target, rollback, outcome, learning, acceptance, value, and boundary artifacts is required.
cognitive_load_removed: Operator no longer has to infer whether provider readiness means production execution is authorized.

capability_matrix.before: Provider readiness evidence was distributed across approval, reproduction, rollback, outcome, learning, acceptance, value, and boundary artifacts.
capability_matrix.after: AOF exposes one controlled execution candidate for operator go/no-go while production execution remains unauthorized.
capability_matrix.user_value: Operators can judge a provider-backed action candidate without manually assembling the chain.

not_proven: This does not prove production provider execution safety, provider semantic correctness, credential or billing safety, actual rollback execution, or market adoption.
